kagyu.label: Kagyu family
kagyu.description: Kagyu lineages, Mahamudra, Naropa-related transmission, and major Kagyu figures.

nyingma.label: Nyingma / Dzogchen
nyingma.description: Nyingma, Dzogchen, Longchen Nyingthig, and Heart-Essence networks.

drukpa.label: Drukpa / Brugpa
drukpa.description: Drukpa Kagyü, Bhutanese Drukpa, and related Bhutan/Ladakh branch material.

items.outcome: Associated with Padmasambhava in the early Great Perfection transmission and with the Vima Nyingthig inherited by Longchenpa.
sourceBooks: Finding Rest in the Nature of the Mind
In that very body, within that very dream
The Words of My Perfect Teacher

items.outcome: Received and safeguarded Heart-Essence teachings later linked to Longchenpa’s lineage narratives.
sourceBooks: Finding Rest in the Nature of the Mind
In that very body, within that very dream
The Words of My Perfect Teacher

items.outcome: Received many transmissions while traveling through India and became Naropa's guru, launching the Kagyu lineage of direct realization.
sourceBooks: In that very body, within that very dream
The Life and Teaching of Naropa
The Life of Marpa the Translator
The Life of the Mahasiddha Tilopa

items.outcome: Received bodhisattva teachings from Serlingpa, journeyed to Tibet, and taught disciples such as Dromtönpa while establishing Kadampa-oriented instruction.
sourceBooks: The Words of My Perfect Teacher

items.outcome: Received Kadampa teachings directly from Atisha and became a major disciple in the transmission to Tibet.

items.outcome: Household base of Marpa and the place where Milarepa first met him and underwent the initial phase of his purification and training.

items.outcome: Hosted early Drukpa institutions, later received Zhabdrung's flight and became the base of the Bhutanese Drukpa school and state formation.
sourceBooks: Finding Rest in the Nature of the Mind

items.outcome: Helped establish Drukpa religious institutions in Bhutan before the later northern/southern branch split.
sourceBooks: Finding Rest in the Nature of the Mind

items.outcome: Hosted early Drukpa institutions, later received Zhabdrung's flight and became the base of the Bhutanese Drukpa school and state formation.; Also hosted Longchenpa during a politically forced exile later remembered in Bhutanese memory.
sourceBooks: Finding Rest in the Nature of the Mind
The Words of My Perfect Teacher

items.outcome: Ordained and studied at Samye, trained for six years at Sangphu Neutog, met Kumaradza at Yartö Khyam in 1335 and received the Vima Nyingthig lineage, and later spent a politically forced period in Bhutan/Bumthang.
sourceBooks: Finding Rest in the Nature of the Mind
The Words of My Perfect Teacher

items.outcome: Ordained and studied at Samye, trained for six years at Sangphu Neutog, met Kumaradza at Yartö Khyam in 1335 and received the Vima Nyingthig lineage, and later spent a politically forced period in Bhutan/Bumthang.

items.outcome: Longchenpa entered Samye as a child novice and became renowned as the well-read scholar from Samye.
